milestones.title: M001: Migration
milestones.vision: A full-stack web application that converts any input (text, URLs, CSV/JSON data) into unique, deterministic algorithmic artwork.

milestones.title: M002: Chromatic Synesthesia Overhaul
milestones.vision: Transform Synesthesia Machine from a technically complete generative art app into a visually compelling art engine whose palettes, renderer behavior, and overall synesthetic expressiveness feel premium, varied, and emotionally intentional.
success_criteria: Repeated real-user generations no longer cluster around the same narrow purple/orange/green-feeling palette family; multiple clearly distinct curated palette families are observable in real outputs.
At least two rendering styles show materially stronger visual expressiveness and art direction in the live results experience, not just in isolated unit tests.
The upgraded color/synesthesia system remains deterministic, contrast-safe, and wired through the existing generation flow for text, URL, and data inputs.
A browser-level end-to-end verification demonstrates the improved artwork quality in the actual app interface.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 -> S02

Produces:
- palette-family selection contract in `src/lib/color/*` with deterministic family identity and richer family metadata
- updated palette-generation invariants covering range, contrast, and family diversity
- test fixtures that prove the new family system spans materially different outputs

Consumes:
- existing deterministic palette primitives, parameter vector schema, and color-module architecture from M001

### S02 -> S03

Produces:
- upgraded parameter-to-aesthetic mapping rules that translate vector behavior into mood/family/composition intent
- stable interfaces consumed by scene builders and results surfaces without breaking existing hooks
- deterministic integration of synesthetic state into render/build paths

Consumes from S01:
- palette-family system, family metadata, and contrast/coherence guarantees

### S03 -> S04

Produces:
- richer scene-builder behavior and/or renderer-specific expression upgrades for at least two styles
- updated thumbnails/results rendering that expose the improved art quality in the live app
- verification-ready visual outputs and fixtures for browser-level proof

Consumes from S01 and S02:
- palette-family outputs and upgraded synesthetic mapping state

milestones.title: M003: Signature Product Experience
milestones.vision: Turn Synesthesia Machine from a technically impressive art generator into a distinct editorial gallery-luxe product whose landing, generation, results, gallery, compare, share, and export experiences feel unmistakably branded, premium, and coherent in real browser use.
success_criteria: A first-time visitor can land on the homepage and understand what Synesthesia Machine is, why it is special, and how to begin within the branded landing/generation surface.
The landing, generation, and results flow feels like one continuous premium experience in the real app rather than a marketing shell followed by a utilitarian tool surface.

Gallery, compare, share, and export surfaces visibly share the same product language — typography, composition, copy posture, action styling, and viewer framing — instead of reading like disconnected pages.
Key redesigned surfaces remain keyboard-usable and preserve existing privacy and diagnostics boundaries while becoming more visually ambitious.

The assembled redesign can be demonstrated end-to-end in a browser through the real entrypoint and major product routes without breaking existing generation, viewer, share, gallery, and compare flows.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:
- a proven editorial direction implemented in the real `src/app/page.tsx` landing/generation/results flow
- upgraded launch-facing copy hierarchy and brand narrative patterns that downstream surfaces can reuse
- stable premium layout/material decisions for `Shell`, `Header`, `InputZone`, `ResultsView`, and the main homepage framing
- truth-tested constraints for how far visual ambition can go without obscuring core generation and diagnostics interactions

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- shared branded shell/chrome language for route-level surfaces built on existing layout boundaries instead of duplicated full-screen wrappers
- normalized shared action/surface/viewer styling that replaces or absorbs brittle undefined utility-class expectations
- reusable product-surface primitives and copy/material patterns that gallery, compare, share, and export-adjacent routes can adopt without rewriting business logic
- a stable cross-surface visual grammar for collector/editorial pages, detail viewers, and action clusters

Consumes:
- S01’s proven landing/results direction, hierarchy, and copy posture as the source of truth for downstream coherence

### S01 → S03

Produces:
- the canonical branded visitor-to-results story that final integration must preserve across gallery/share/compare routes
- real homepage and results proof surfaces that establish the acceptance bar for cross-route coherence

Consumes:
- nothing directly beyond S01 being the first shipped expression of the milestone direction

milestones.title: M004: Product Coherence and Continuity — Context

slices.title: Parameter System Color
slices.demo: Build the quantile-based normalization engine, weighted-blend parameter mapping tables, and provenance generation system.
slices.goal: Build the quantile-based normalization engine, weighted-blend parameter mapping tables, and provenance generation system.

slices.title: Text Analysis Input Ui
slices.demo: Build the real text analyzer that replaces `extractMockSignals` with genuine NLP features: AFINN-165 sentiment, syllable counting via the `syllable` package, and all statistical text features.
slices.goal: Build the real text analyzer that replaces `extractMockSignals` with genuine NLP features: AFINN-165 sentiment, syllable counting via the `syllable` package, and all statistical text features.

slices.title: Geometric Renderer Canvas Ui
slices.demo: Build the pure-function geometric composition engine that transforms a ParameterVector + PaletteResult into a deterministic SceneGraph of drawing instructions, with all composition laws enforced.
slices.goal: Build the pure-function geometric composition engine that transforms a ParameterVector + PaletteResult into a deterministic SceneGraph of drawing instructions, with all composition laws enforced.

slices.title: Export & Accessibility
slices.demo: unit tests prove Export & Accessibility works
slices.goal: unit tests prove Export & Accessibility works

slices.title: Palette Family System
slices.demo: deterministic generations can produce multiple clearly different curated palette families with test-level proof that repetition has materially dropped.
slices.goal: Establish a deterministic curated palette-family system that materially reduces palette repetition while preserving contrast safety and the existing generation entrypoints.

slices.title: Synesthetic Mapping Upgrade
slices.demo: parameter behavior drives palette-family and mood selection more intentionally, with observable changes in real generated outputs.
slices.goal: Upgrade the shared color mapping seam so the full parameter vector deterministically produces richer synesthetic intent that materially changes palette realization and exposes stable diagnostics for downstream renderers and verification.

slices.title: Renderer Expressiveness Pass
slices.demo: at least two styles render noticeably richer, more premium compositions in the live results surface while preserving determinism.
slices.goal: Make at least two renderer styles visibly consume `PaletteResult.mapping` so live results feel richer, more premium, and more art-directed without breaking determinism or collapsing style identity.

slices.title: Live Art Quality Integration Proof
slices.demo: Status note: Closed with real browser proof for text/data, restored no-DB build health, and explicit blocked-state diagnostics for the remaining URL runtime dependency.
slices.goal: Prove in the actual browser experience that the upgraded M002 art system surfaces materially richer, less repetitive, more art-directed live outputs across the existing text, URL, and data flows, with durable evidence suitable for milestone closeout.

slices.title: Editorial landing, generation, and results journey
slices.demo: a visitor can use the real homepage flow to understand the product, generate artwork, and land in a premium branded results experience that already feels continuous rather than split between marketing and tool UI.
slices.goal: Redesign the real homepage flow so first-time visitors can understand Synesthesia Machine, generate from the existing text/URL/data entry seams, and land in a premium editorial results experience that preserves diagnostics, privacy, and keyboard-usable core actions.

slices.title: Shared brand system across shell, actions, and viewer surfaces
slices.demo: the redesigned visual language is no longer homepage-only — shared chrome, action treatments, viewer framing, copy posture, and route-level surface patterns visibly unify results and the reusable product seams downstream pages depend on.
slices.goal: Propagate the S01 editorial gallery-luxe system into the reusable downstream seams — route shells, browse controls, compare mode, and gallery/share viewers — so the product no longer falls back to disconnected page-local wrappers or undefined utility classes once the visitor leaves the homepage/results flow.

slices.title: Cohesive gallery, compare, share, and export product family
slices.demo: gallery, compare, share, and export-adjacent flows can be demoed in the browser as one coherent collector/editorial product family, with final integration proof that the milestone works end-to-end through real routes.
slices.goal: Finish M003’s primary coherence contract by making the results action family and downstream gallery/compare/share/export-adjacent routes read as one collector/editorial product in code, tests, and real browser use.

decisions.choice: next/font/google for Geist
decisions.rationale: Matches project scaffold and avoids extra font-loading complexity

decisions.choice: Alea via seedrandom
decisions.rationale: Fast, deterministic, and already integrated across render paths

decisions.choice: Ban Math.random() in render/pipeline code
decisions.rationale: Determinism is a product promise, not an implementation detail

decisions.choice: Dark mode default with persisted user choice
decisions.rationale: Dark backgrounds better frame generated artwork
decisions.revisable: Yes — if future brand direction changes

decisions.choice: Quantile-based scaling against calibration corpus
decisions.rationale: Produces more expressive stable distributions than min/max normalization

decisions.choice: OKLCH via culori
decisions.rationale: Best fit for perceptual palette control and contrast-safe tuning

decisions.choice: Module-level cached calibration in client hooks
decisions.rationale: Avoids recalculating calibration data per render/generation

decisions.choice: Pure scene-graph builders plus style-specific canvas components
decisions.rationale: Keeps composition deterministic and testable while isolating Canvas side effects

decisions.choice: Route handlers call boundary modules (`db-cache`, `db-gallery`) instead of inline DB code
decisions.rationale: Improves isolation, testing, and future refactor safety

decisions.choice: Scene graphs carry source ParameterVector
decisions.rationale: Enables deterministic alt text and export diagnostics without recomputing pipeline state

decisions.choice: Use a design-first multi-milestone sequence (M002 art engine, M003 full-site redesign, M004 product coherence, M005 launch hardening)
decisions.rationale: Matches user intent to expand ambition rather than shrink to an MVP cleanup

decisions.choice: Editorial gallery luxe
decisions.rationale: User wants super artsy, sleek, premium presentation rather than neutral SaaS polish
decisions.revisable: Yes — if user changes art direction

decisions.choice: Wide curated deterministic palette families
decisions.rationale: Directly addresses the user complaint that current outputs feel stuck in repetitive purple/orange/green bands

decisions.choice: M002 prioritizes palette + renderer expressiveness before full-site redesign
decisions.rationale: Better artwork quality should precede broader visual shell redesign so later polish showcases stronger output

decisions.choice: Treat Synesthesia Machine as a distinct branded art product, not just a nicer portfolio demo
decisions.rationale: User wants a full product and public launch readiness, not only cosmetic cleanup

decisions.choice: Optimize for public portfolio launch
decisions.rationale: Balances ambition with realistic launch scope; does not require full SaaS commercialization yet
decisions.revisable: Yes — if user later pursues commercial productization

decisions.choice: Add explicit family-selection and family-diversity tests before implementation
decisions.rationale: S01 must prove reduced repetition through executable contract tests rather than subjective review or metadata-only assertions

decisions.choice: Split `generatePalette()` into deterministic family selection plus family-specific realization while preserving the existing shared call shape
decisions.rationale: Keeps text/URL/data integration stable while creating the boundary S02 needs for mood-driven mapping upgrades

decisions.choice: Expose stable family metadata on `PaletteResult` for inspection and downstream consumption
decisions.rationale: Gives future agents and later slices direct visibility into palette-family outcomes without reproducing hidden branch logic
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a better shared diagnostic surface emerges

decisions.choice: Store family-specific hue/chroma/lightness shaping coefficients in `palette-families.ts` and drive realization from data rather than branching logic
decisions.rationale: Keeps curated family behavior inspectable and tunable while preserving the shared `generatePalette()` seam
decisions.revisable: Yes — if later slices need a richer aesthetic model

decisions.choice: Do not mark S01 complete unless family-selection, family-diversity, and baseline palette tests pass together
decisions.rationale: Prevents metadata-only progress from being mistaken for a safe palette-family system

decisions.choice: Add a first-class deterministic synesthetic mapping artifact consumed by `generatePalette()` while preserving the shared `generatePalette(vector, seed)` seam
decisions.rationale: Gives S02 a real full-vector aesthetic-intent boundary and creates the downstream contract S03 can consume without changing hook entrypoints

decisions.choice: Expose additive mapping diagnostics on `PaletteResult` and prove them through hook integration tests
decisions.rationale: Makes mood/family/harmony intent inspectable at the shared palette seam and across text/URL/data flows instead of hiding behavior in implementation details
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a better cross-slice diagnostics surface emerges

decisions.choice: Treat hook-level determinism as canonical-input stability of vector and mapping diagnostics rather than strict full-palette equality when seed derivation is not canonicalized
decisions.rationale: Keeps integration tests aligned with the real runtime contract and localizes the remaining determinism gap to seed derivation instead of writing false guarantees
decisions.revisable: Yes — if later work canonicalizes data-path seed derivation

decisions.choice: Introduce one shared pure renderer-expression interpretation seam from `PaletteResult.mapping`, then have organic and typographic scene builders consume it instead of re-deriving mood from raw params
decisions.rationale: Preserves cross-style coherence, keeps scene builders deterministic/testable, and makes mapping-driven art direction inspectable at the scene-graph boundary
decisions.revisable: Yes — if later slices require a richer shared art-direction contract

decisions.choice: Use mapping-aware renderer scene tests plus existing ResultsView/StyleSelector and hook integration suites as the slice gate before browser-level art proof
decisions.rationale: Proves two styles visibly benefit from the S02 mapping seam while staying honest that S04 still owns live browser acceptance

decisions.choice: Expose applied expressiveness posture directly on organic and typographic scene graphs
decisions.rationale: Keeps mapping-driven art direction inspectable at the runtime scene boundary and lets future slices localize issues without reproducing hidden renderer logic
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a better cross-renderer inspection surface emerges

decisions.choice: Define S04 completion with failing acceptance tests, restored `npm run build`, and browser-level assertions/artifacts across text, URL, and data flows
decisions.rationale: Keeps milestone closeout tied to executable and browser-visible proof instead of subjective screenshots or test-only claims

decisions.choice: Expose concise derived family/mapping/expressiveness diagnostics in the real results surface without revealing raw inputs
decisions.rationale: Gives future agents a trustworthy first inspection seam for weak live outputs while preserving privacy boundaries in durable proof artifacts
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a better launch-facing diagnostics surface emerges

decisions.choice: Defer DB-backed route/gallery imports behind runtime guards so `next build` can complete without `DATABASE_URL` in local proof mode
decisions.rationale: Keeps slice verification focused on the art-proof/runtime seam while preserving explicit 503 failure visibility for DB-backed routes when the database is intentionally unavailable
decisions.revisable: Yes — if local build environments always provide database credentials

decisions.choice: Treat S04 as complete once text/data browser proof and build health pass and the remaining URL runtime dependency is explicitly surfaced with actionable diagnostics
decisions.rationale: Keeps milestone closeout honest without hiding real blockers, while avoiding a false binary where integrated proof is discarded despite a trustworthy failure surface
decisions.revisable: Yes — if URL analysis becomes fully DB-optional later

decisions.choice: Plan M003 around the existing `page.tsx` landing/results seam first, then shared shell/action/viewer primitives, then final gallery/compare/share/export integration
decisions.rationale: The hardest milestone risk is whether one editorial direction survives the real landing-to-results journey; proving that first creates a stable branded language downstream routes can consume instead of fragmenting into page-by-page redesigns
decisions.revisable: Yes — if implementation reveals a different cross-surface dependency order

decisions.choice: Treat R003, R004, and R009 as the core M003 contract, with R005 and R010 partially advanced but not fully closed in this milestone
decisions.rationale: Keeps M003 focused on premium product experience and coherent browser-visible redesign while preserving launch hardening and full accessibility breadth for their existing later owners
decisions.revisable: Yes — if milestone scope is renegotiated

decisions.choice: Define S01 completion with a failing-then-passing homepage editorial flow test, preserved ResultsView live-proof contract, passing local build, and real browser verification including honest URL no-DB failure-path inspection
decisions.rationale: Keeps the highest-risk landing/results seam tied to executable and runtime truth instead of subjective design review alone

decisions.choice: Treat homepage progress state, ResultsView proof diagnostics, and visible action/error states as the canonical first inspection surfaces for the redesigned landing/results journey
decisions.rationale: Gives future agents a stable browser-visible path to localize generation and continuity problems without exposing raw input
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a stronger shared diagnostics surface is introduced later

decisions.choice: Express the homepage redesign through shared editorial shell/panel/support/action utilities in `globals.css` instead of page-local classes alone
decisions.rationale: Keeps the branded intake surface reusable for downstream results/shell work and avoids another isolated one-off hero treatment
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a broader design-token layer supersedes these utilities later

decisions.choice: Extend the same editorial token layer to results-only cards, chips, modal shells, and form fields rather than relying on undefined semantic utility classes
decisions.rationale: Preserves landing→results continuity, removes styling drift in export/share/save surfaces, and gives future slices reusable branded primitives for adjacent routes
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a more formal design-token system replaces these utilities later

decisions.choice: Define S02 completion with a failing-then-passing shared route-surface test, preserved compare/gallery/share contracts, passing build, and browser verification across browse/compare/detail routes including truthful unavailable-state inspection
decisions.rationale: Keeps cross-surface coherence tied to executable and runtime truth rather than subjective visual review, and ensures failure-path messaging stays visible while the design system propagates

decisions.choice: Normalize gallery browse, compare, gallery detail, and share detail onto shared editorial shell, route-intro, and viewer scaffolds built from the S01 token layer instead of page-local wrappers or a second design system
decisions.rationale: Directly addresses the route-fragmentation risk identified in research, removes undefined compare utilities, and creates reusable branded seams S03 can finish integrating
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a later formal component system supersedes these primitives

decisions.choice: Define S03 completion with one assembled route-family test, preserved export/share/gallery contracts, passing build, and real browser verification from homepage results into gallery/compare/share-detail or truthful unavailable states
decisions.rationale: Keeps R004 tied to executable and browser-visible family continuity instead of isolated page polish, while explicitly rechecking keyboard/accessibility-aware behavior and DB-truthful diagnostics

decisions.choice: Reuse the same collector/editorial phrases across results actions and downstream compare/share/gallery viewers while keeping each route’s truth boundary explicit
decisions.rationale: Closes the product-family gap without collapsing distinct contracts: share remains parameter-only, gallery hints remain optional, and export copy stays honest about current backend limits
decisions.revisable: Yes — if later brand language evolves
